data.title: ARTIFACTS_DIR mismatch: reviewer sub-agent writes reviewer-findings.json to its own ARTIFACTS_DIR hash, not the target worktree's
data.description: When a code-reviewer sub-agent is dispatched and it writes reviewer-findings.json, it uses get_artifacts_dir() which hashes its own REPO_ROOT (the sub-agent's working directory). The orchestrator's record-review.sh reads from the orchestrator's ARTIFACTS_DIR (different hash). This means the file is never found unless the orchestrator manually copies it between directories. Required workaround every review cycle during epic 615f-fad3: compute sha256 of reviewer REPO_ROOT to find /tmp/workflow-plugin-<hash>/, copy reviewer-findings.json to the target worktree's /tmp/workflow-plugin-<target-hash>/. Fix: record-review.sh should accept an explicit --findings-file path argument, OR reviewer sub-agents should be dispatched with WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR set to the orchestrator's ARTIFACTS_DIR.
